title: Hierarchical Episodic Memory for Autonomous Coding Agents
extractedContent:
# Hierarchical Episodic Memory for Autonomous Coding Agents

Autonomous coding agents struggle to maintain context over large, multi-session programming tasks. This paper proposes a Hierarchical Episodic Memory (HEM) architecture to summarize, index, and retrieve past agent actions and system traces.

## Mechanism
HEM parses raw action logs into low-level trace blocks, aggregates blocks into episodic memories using sliding window summarizers, and creates a semantic index. When an agent executes a command, the memory retriever returns both short-term trace details and long-term episodic summaries.

## Findings
HEM reduces context window token usage by 40% while improving task success rates by 25% on complex software engineering benchmarks.

## Limitations
- The summarization stage introduces extra token cost
- Higher index building latency during command loops
- Relies on accurate vector lookup indices
